agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to replace 1965 Zoning Bylaw with new 2026 version
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to repeal Burnaby's current Zoning Bylaw (from 1965) and replace it with a new Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026. This proposed bylaw would also introduce new Development Permit Area Guidelines and reassign some properties to different zoning districts. If approved, this would change the rules for what you can build and how you can use your property in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to speak up about how the new rules would affect your property.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1
BURNABY ZONING BYLAW - PROPOSED REPEAL AND REPLACEMENT
Purpose: To seek Council approval to repeal Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965 and replace it with the proposed Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 2026, including new Development Permit Area Guidelines and zoning district reassignment.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Housing Authority Year 2 operating funding request
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider approving the Burnaby Housing Authority's request for Year 2 operating funding. This is a proposal to provide money to the Housing Authority to help cover its day-to-day costs for the second year of operation. The decision has not been made yet — council will vote on whether to approve this funding at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2
BHA - ASSISTANCE REQUEST FOR YEAR 2 OPERATING FUNDING
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation’s assistance request for Year 2 Operating Funding.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 2026 property tax rates for Burnaby businesses
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on the proposed 2026 property tax rates for municipal, regional, and Business Improvement Area (BIA) properties. If approved, this would set the tax rates that commercial and industrial property owners in Burnaby will pay next year. The decision will affect how much you owe in property taxes for 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.3
2026 PROPERTY TAX RATES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for 2026 municipal, regional and business improvement area (BIA) property tax rates.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to appoint an Approving Officer
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to appoint an Approving Officer. An Approving Officer is a person who has the authority to approve or reject plans for new subdivisions (splitting land into smaller lots) and road closures. This decision is about who will hold that official role, not about changing any rules. If approved, the appointed officer will handle these land-use decisions for the city.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action needed from you right now. Watch for the council decision if you have a pending subdivision or road-closure application.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4
APPOINTMENT OF APPROVING OFFICER
Purpose: To seek Council approval to appoint an Approving Officer.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on retaining wall height variance at 8012 11th Ave
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a Development Variance Permit (DVP) for a property at 8012 11th Avenue. A DVP is a special permission that lets a property owner break certain rules in the zoning bylaw. In this case, the owner wants to build retaining walls that are taller than what the current rules allow. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider the request at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or develop property near 8012 11th Avenue, watch for the council decision — it could set a precedent for similar height variances in your area.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5
DVP #26-01 - 8012 11TH AVENUE - RETAINING WALLS HEIGHT VARIANCES
Purpose: To seek Council approval for Development Variance Permit (DVP) #26-01.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to award insurance contracts for property and liability programs
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on awarding contracts for the city's annual property and liability insurance programs. These contracts cover insurance for city-owned buildings, equipment, and liability protection. A decision is expected at the next council meeting. If approved, the contracts will take effect for the 2026 insurance year.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.6
2026 INSURANCE PROGRAMS - CONTRACT RENEWALS
Purpose: To request approval to award insurance contracts for the annual property and liability insurance programs.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve Heritage Commission members attending 2026 BC conference
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of Burnaby's Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC, from May 4-6, 2026. The CHC advises Council on heritage matters, and this trip would help members learn about best practices and network with other heritage professionals. The decision has not been made yet — Council will consider whether to approve the travel and related costs at an upcoming meeting.

key_dates.label: 2026 Heritage BC Conference in Squamish — proposed attendance dates
2026 Heritage BC Conference in Squamish — proposed attendance dates

agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.7
2026 HERITAGE BC CONFERENCE
Purpose: To seek Council approval for members of the Community Heritage Commission (CHC) to attend the 2026 Annual Heritage BC Conference in Squamish, BC from May 4-6, 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract award for networking software licenses
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses. If approved, this would authorize the city to purchase the software licenses needed to keep its computer networks running. This is a routine procurement decision that would allow the city to maintain its internal technology systems.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.8
CA - NETWORKING SOFTWARE LICENSES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of networking software licenses.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on furniture contract for new civic facility
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract award for the supply, delivery, and installation of office and facility furniture for the new civic facility. If approved, this contract would cover all the furniture and equipment needed to fit out the new building. The decision is on the agenda for the upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.9
CA - NEW CIVIC FACILITY FIT-OUT, FURNISHINGS AND EQUIPMENT
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply, delivery and installation of office and facility furniture.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for external audit services
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed change to the city's contract for external audit services. This is not a final decision yet. If approved, the change would modify the existing agreement with the company that audits the city's financial records. The purpose is to ensure the city's finances are reviewed properly. The specific details of the contract change are on the council agenda for discussion and a vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - EXTERNAL AUDIT SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for External Audit Services.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for geotechnical work at Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal amendment to an existing agreement) for geotechnical consulting services at the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex. Geotechnical consultants study soil and ground conditions to ensure buildings are safe and stable. If approved, this change would adjust the scope or cost of the consultant's work on the project. The decision has not been made yet — council will consider it at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No action required from you at this time. Watch for the council decision if your business is involved in the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - BURNABY LAKE RECREATION COMPLEX GEOTECHNICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for geotechnical consultant services for the Burnaby Lake Recreation Complex project.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to vote on Spring 2026 community grant recipients
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving the recommended recipients for the Spring 2026 community grant program. This is a proposed decision — if approved, the grants would provide funding to local community groups and non-profits. The vote will happen at an upcoming council meeting. Business owners who partner with or rely on community organizations should watch for the outcome, as it could affect local services and events.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.1
ECC - SPRING 2026 COMMUNITY GRANT APPLICATIONS
Purpose: To seek Council approval for the recommended grant recipients for the Spring 2026 intake.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Mid-year 2026 fee changes to Burnaby Fees and Charges Bylaw
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider mid-year 2026 amendments to the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would update certain fees the city charges for various services and permits. If approved, the new fees would take effect later in 2026. The exact fees and which services are affected will be detailed in the council meeting agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if you hold a city licence or permit, check the final fee schedule to see if your costs change.

key_dates.label: Council meeting to vote on proposed fee amendments

agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.2
FMC - BURNABY CONSOLIDATED FEES AND CHARGES BYLAW - 2026 MID-YEAR AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To propose mid-year 2026 fee amendments to certain fee schedules within the Burnaby Consolidated Fees and Charges Bylaw.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw up for council vote
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed Tenant Protection Framework and a new Tenant Protection Bylaw. If approved, this bylaw would create new rules for landlords, including limits on rent increases above the provincial guideline and stronger protections against evictions for renovations. The goal is to help renters stay in their homes. Council will consider the proposal at an upcoming meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — this bylaw could affect how you manage rental properties in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.3
PDC - PROPOSED TENANT PROTECTION FRAMEWORK AND TENANT PROTECTION BYLAW AND ASSOCIATED BYLAW AMENDMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement of the proposed tenant protection framework and Burnaby Tenant Protection Bylaw and associated bylaw amendments.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on Southeast Bikeway Upgrade improvements
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed improvements for the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project. If approved, this project would add new bike lanes, crossings, and safety upgrades along the route. The decision is not final yet — Council will discuss and vote on the plan at an upcoming meeting. Business owners near the bikeway should be aware that construction could affect traffic, parking, and customer access during the build.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision date — if approved, prepare for possible construction impacts on your business access and parking.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.4
TC - SOUTHEAST BIKEWAY UPGRADE PROJECT - PROPOSED IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To seek Council approval of the proposed improvements as part of the Southeast Bikeway Upgrade Project.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update design standards for safer intersections
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to update the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards. The change would require new street designs to include 'protected intersection geometry' — a safer layout that separates pedestrians and cyclists from turning vehicles. If approved, this would apply to new developments and road projects in the Town Centre area. The goal is to make walking and biking safer for people of all ages and abilities.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect your development or construction project in Burnaby's Town Centre.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics
agenda_briefs.source_body:
8.5
TC - PROPOSED AMENDMENT TO THE BURNABY TOWN CENTRE DESIGN STANDARDS TO INCORPORATE PROTECTED INTERSECTION GEOMETRY
Purpose: To seek Council endorsement to amend the Burnaby Town Centre Design Standards to incorporate protected intersection geometry, improve road safety for pedestrians and cyclists and support all-ages-and-abilities mobility.

agenda_briefs.title: Burnaby staff recommend against exempting Brentwood Park from SSMUH rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a staff report recommending against exempting Brentwood Park properties from provincial Small-Scale Multi-Unit Housing (SSMUH) requirements. Staff say the City is bound by provincial law to zone for SSMUH, and restrictive covenants are not a legal exemption. A proposed alternative — amending the Building Bylaw city-wide to require compliance with restrictive covenants — is not recommended due to added costs, process delays, and impacts on residential, commercial, and industrial properties across Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for council's decision on April 28, 2026 — if council rejects staff's recommendation, the exemption may still proceed.

key_dates.label: Council receives staff report on Brentwood Park SSMUH exemption

agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.1
BRENTWOOD PARK SSMUH MOTION - RESTRICTIVE COVENANTS AND STATUTORY BUILDING SCHEMES
Purpose: To respond to Council’s September 9, 2025, motion regarding exempting Brentwood Park properties with Restrictive Covenants from Provincial SSMUH requirements.

attachments.title: Brentwood Park SSMUH Motion - Restrictive Covenants and Statutory Building Schem

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive building permit activity report for March 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will receive a report on building permits issued in Burnaby from March 1 to March 31, 2026. This is an information item only — no vote or decision is required. The report gives a snapshot of construction activity in the city, including the number and types of permits issued. It does not propose any new rules or changes.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.2
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 03 FROM MARCH 1 - MARCH 31, 2026
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Heritage designation for 3789 Royal Oak Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider an 'as-found report' and 'Statement of Significance' for the property at 3789 Royal Oak Avenue. A Statement of Significance is a document that explains why a building or site is historically important. This is the first step toward possibly designating the property as a municipal heritage resource. If designated, future changes to the building or land would need special approval. The decision has not been made yet — Council is reviewing the information now.
agenda_briefs.action_required: If you own or manage this property, watch for the next Council meeting — you may want to attend or submit comments before a final vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.3
CHC - AS-FOUND REPORT AND STATEMENT OF SIGNIFICANCE FOR HERITAGE RESOURCES AT 3789 ROYAL OAK AVENUE
Purpose: To provide Council an as-found report and Statement of Significance for heritage resources located at 7389 Royal Oak Avenue for information.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby solid waste and recycling 2025 performance report
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a summary of the City of Burnaby's solid waste and recycling collection system performance for 2025. This report covers how well the city's garbage, recycling, and organics pickup services are working. A proposed bylaw or policy change could follow based on this report, but no decision has been made yet. Business owners who generate waste or recycling should watch for any upcoming changes to collection rules or fees.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this report — it could lead to changes in waste collection rules or fees that affect your business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Retail, Grocery & Food Retail
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.4
EC - SOLID WASTE AND RECYCLING (SWR) 2025 ANNUAL REPORT
Purpose: To provide Council with a summary of the City of Burnaby solid waste and recycling collection system performance.

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider update on food sustainability measures in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive an operational update on food sustainability measures. This is a report to Council, not a final decision. The update will cover current programs and actions the City is taking to improve food sustainability. No new bylaws or rules are being proposed at this time. Council is set to review the information and may give direction to staff for future steps.
agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Agriculture
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.5
EC - SU: ENHANCING FOOD SUSTAINABILITY IN BURNABY
Purpose: To provide Council with an operational update on food sustainability measures within the City of Burnaby.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Safety improvements at Central Boulevard and Metropolis parkade intersection
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and the Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access. The proposal aims to make the area safer for drivers, pedestrians, and cyclists. A vote on whether to move forward with these changes is expected at an upcoming council meeting.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
9.6
TC - CENTRAL BOULEVARD AND PARKADE INERSECTION SAFETY IMPROVEMENTS
Purpose: To provide Council with an overview of the proposed safety improvements at the intersection of Central Boulevard and Metropolis at Metrotown underground parkade access.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezone four Burnaby properties for taller towers and a hotel
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw amendment that would change the zoning for four properties near the intersection of Grand Promenade, Austin Road, and Gatineau Place. The developer wants to replace some planned office space with a hotel and restaurant, add 362 more market strata (condo) units, and make the two residential towers taller — Tower 1B would go from 80 to 87 storeys, and Tower 1A from 73 to 77 storeys. The proposal also changes how the developer meets the city's requirement for affordable housing (called the 'inclusionary requirement') by only providing it for Phase 1 and moving that obligation to a different site (the Carrigan site). This is an amendment to a rezoning that was already approved in September 2024.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit a written comment to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14808 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 7, 2026 - REZ #24-23 (3900 Grand Promenade, 6950 Austin Rd., 9858 & 9898 Gatineau Place)
Purpose: to amend the previous rezoning of the subject lands pursuant to REZ #18-32 (Bylaw No. 14447), which received Final Adoption on September 23, 2024, and included two market strata residential buildings of 80 (Tower 1B) and 73 (Tower 1A) storeys, respectively, inclusive of a 13-storey retail and office podium. As part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to replace a portion of the commercial office use with hotel and restaurant uses, and add 362 additional market strata units from the development concept approved under REZ #18-32, resulting in an additional seven storeys for Tower 1B and four storeys for Tower 1A. In addition, as part of the subject amendment rezoning application, the applicant is proposing to provide the inclusionary requirement associated with Phase 1 only, to be transferred to the Carrigan site, which is an amendment from the previous rezoning approval.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: 12-storey mixed-use development at 6535 Kingsway
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone 6535 Kingsway. The plan would allow a 12-storey building with 290 residential units, ground-floor shops facing Kingsway, and underground parking. This proposal matches the city's Official Community Plan and the Edmonds Community Plan. The decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or submit written comments to Burnaby City Council before the vote.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.2
#14809 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 8, 2026 - REZ #25-09 (6535 Kingsway)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a 12-storey mixed-use multiple-family development with a double-height commercial storey at street level comprised of 290 residential units, ground-oriented commercial uses fronting Kingsway, and underground parking. The proposal is consistent with the applicable OCP and the Edmonds Community Plan designations for the site.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Five-storey self-storage building at 2757 Norland Avenue
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw amendment to rezone 2757 Norland Avenue. If approved, the change would allow construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal follows the 1998 Official Community Plan and approved transition rules. This is not yet a done deal — the decision is up for a vote at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date and attend to voice your support or concerns.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.1.3
#14810 - Burnaby Zoning Bylaw 1965, Amendment Bylaw No. 9, 2026 - REZ #25-21 (2757 Norland Avenue)
Purpose: to permit the construction of a five-storey enclosed self-storage building. The proposal is being advanced in accordance with the applicable 1998 OCP policies and approved transitionary provisions.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot program
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed amendment to Burnaby's Street and Traffic Bylaw that would create a pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, the pilot would allow these devices on certain streets and paths under new rules. The amendment is still under consideration and has not been enacted yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how you operate if your business uses or serves e-bikes or e-scooters.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.1
#14811 - Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1961, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend Burnaby Street and Traffic Bylaw 1964 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby e-bike and e-scooter pilot in parks
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would allow e-bikes and electric kick scooters in certain Burnaby parks as part of a pilot program. The amendment to the Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 would create new rules for where and how these devices can be used. This is not yet law — the decision is up for approval at a future council meeting.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments if you use e-bikes or e-scooters in parks.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.2
#14812 - Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979, Amendment Bylaw No. 1, 2026
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Parks Regulation Bylaw 1979 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby bylaw amendment for e-bike and e-scooter pilot enforcement
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would update the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw to support a new pilot program for e-bikes and electric kick scooters. If approved, this change would allow the city to issue tickets and enforce rules specifically for these devices during the pilot. The amendment is needed to make sure the city can properly manage and enforce the rules of the pilot program.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect how e-bikes and e-scooters are regulated in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.3
#14813 - Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009, Amendment Bylaw No. 3, 2026 (e-bike and electric kick scooter programs)
Purpose: to amend the Burnaby Bylaw Notice Enforcement Bylaw 2009 (e-bike and electric kick scooter pilot program)

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026 sets new property tax rates
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed bylaw to set the 2026 tax rates for all taxable land and buildings in Burnaby. This bylaw, called the Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026, is required under sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter (the main law that gives BC cities their powers). If approved, it will determine how much property tax you pay next year based on your property's assessed value. The decision has not been made yet — council is set to vote on this proposal.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote on this bylaw — it will directly affect your 2026 property tax bill.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail, Professional Services
agenda_briefs.source_body:
11.2.4
#14814 - Burnaby Rates Bylaw 2026
Purpose: to establish tax rates upon all taxable land and improvements pursuant to sections 197 and 216 of the Community Charter
